div1.entryFree: Bristle, vb., to erect as the swine does its hair: boar with --d hair, Mids. II, 2, 31. doth dogged war b. his angry crest, John IV, 3, 149. b. up the crest of youth, H4A I, 1, 98. b. thy courage up, H5 II, 3, 5 (Pistol's speech). with his Amazonian chin he drove the --d lips before him, Cor. II, 2, 96 (== bearded).
